dish_name: Al Pastor Pineapple Crunch Taco Plate
tagline: Achiote-chipotle chicken, charred corn, and a smoky habanero kick — built on crispy corn shells.
ingredients: chicken al pastor (LTO — pineapple, achiote, chipotle)
corn salsa (mild — corn, poblano, red onion, jalapeño, lime)
tomatillo red chili salsa extra hot (habanero)
guacamole (avocado, lime, cilantro, red onion, jalapeño)
signal_rank_note: Al pastor is a strong signal in the Mission, but Chipotle's LTO version will always rank below El Farolito and taqueria-row competitors who do trompo al pastor daily.
novelty_check: Chipotle has no fixed dish list; this leans into the al pastor LTO as a crispy-taco plate format rather than the default burrito/bowl assembly, pairing it with the extra-hot habanero salsa for a Mission-calibrated heat profile.
confidence_reason: Al pastor scores 0.58 (strong) and is already in pantry, but the Mission's taqueria density caps how much credit a chain version earns — honest deliverable, capped ceiling.

dish_potential: A Mole Bowl or Mole Burrito line — barbacoa or chicken finished in real mole poblano over cilantro-lime rice, with queso blanco and pickled onion. Could also support a seasonal Mole Enchilada-style burrito tied to Día de los Muertos.
operational_lift: Mole ships as a heat-and-hold sauce in the existing steam-table well — line-friendly with no new station, but sourcing a credible mole (not a chili gravy) is the real lift.
comp_context: Nopalito and La Oaxaqueña on Mission St are the local benchmarks — La Oaxaqueña's mole negro is the reference point a Chipotle version would be measured against.

tagline: Earthy black lentils, harissa-honey chicken, and bright sumac slaw over saffron rice.
signal_rank_note: Lentils are a notable but not breakout signal in the Mission — this is a durable build, not a trend grab.
novelty_check: CAVA is assembly-line with no fixed dish list; this build foregrounds black lentils as the hero (not a side ingredient) and pairs them with sumac slaw + tahini for a layered tart-earthy profile rather than the usual hummus-forward bowl.
confidence_reason: All four ship-now signals (lentils, tahini, harissa, sumac) score below 0.35 in this neighborhood — honest but not high-ceiling. The dish ships cleanly from pantry with zero new SKUs.

dish_potential: A Charred Cauliflower bowl or pita — harissa-roasted cauliflower as a standalone vegetable protein, paired with tahini, pickled onions, and crazy feta. Opens a credible vegan/vegetarian centerpiece beyond falafel.
operational_lift: Cauliflower roasts on the same sheet pans as the existing roasted vegetables — minimal line complexity, just a new prep SKU and a hold position.
comp_context: Souvla and Tawla are winning charred/whole-roasted cauliflower in SF right now; Lazy Bear has run cauliflower as a center-of-plate vegetable course that the Mission audience already knows.

tagline: Miso-glazed salmon, pesto-dressed greens, and crispy rice — umami meets basil meets crunch.
signal_rank_note: Pesto scores very high in the Mission and this build keeps it honest — real basil + pesto vinaigrette, not a green-gesture.
novelty_check: Distinct from Chicken Pesto Parm (chicken/parm-led, Italian frame) and Miso Glazed Salmon (miso-led, no pesto). This bowl explicitly crosses pesto × miso on the same plate with crispy rice texture — a combination not in the 27-dish list.
confidence_reason: Two of Sweetgreen's highest-signal flavors (pesto 0.85, miso 0.83) both already in pantry, combined in a format the brand hasn't shipped. Mission audience is fusion-tolerant per local context.

dish_potential: A Mole Chicken Bowl — roasted chicken finished in mole, over wild rice with corn salsa, avocado, pickled onions, and tortilla chips. Opens a Mexican-leaning line that Sweetgreen currently only gestures at via Fish Taco and corn salsa.
operational_lift: Mole as a finishing sauce ladled at assembly — fits the existing line flow, but requires a hot-hold position and a credible mole supplier (not a generic chili sauce).
comp_context: La Oaxaqueña and Nopalito anchor mole in the Mission; Tartine Manufactory has run mole-adjacent plates that Sweetgreen's craft-curious customer already buys into.
